game.short_text: Sail upwards through tiny raindrops as a tardigrade on a quest to go higher!
game.title: Upgrade

details.title: Upgrade
details.description:
Created for the Gamedev.tv 2025 Game Jam!
Embark on a gigantic journey of microscopic proportions as you control a tardigrade that has been lifted into the air by an updraft.Dodge falling raindrops to reach new heights but remember that in the air tardigrades dry out and go into stasis, making it harder to move. Stasis can be reversed by hitting a raindrop at which point you enter an even tinier aquatic world where you swim slowly, feasting on amoeba and nematodes while avoiding perils that can harm you. While you're in a raindrop you begin falling back towards the ground so don't spend too much time in one! The game ends when you hit the ground or fail to survive in the raindrops. Controls 
While in the air: A + D to move horizontally and avoid raindropsWhile in the water: A + W + D to swim around


Release History
0.9.5 - Fixed bug where game over screen wasn't being displayed.0.9.4 - Pause screen during gameplay and beastiary on the about screen0.9.3 - Official first release for the game jam
Future Potential Upgrades
Pause screenImproved raindrop spacing to prevent overlapsAbility to kill hunter tardigrades with microplasticsRare hats for hunter tardigrades (that you can collect if you kill them)A larger variety of microorganisms to help/hinder the playerBetter balancing.An animation/transition for when you hit the ground.

Credits
Programming - Clayton RumleyArtwork - KaraMusic - Music from https://filmmusic.io"Jellyfish in Space" by Kevin MacLeod (https://incompetech.com)License: CC BY (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)Special thanks to Waijee for testing! Play his game entry, The World Tortoise and the Hare!

game.short_text: Evolve your Cell to Survive (Casual Clicker Game)
game.title: Petri Dish - GDTV Jam 25

details.title: Petri Dish - GDTV Jam 25
details.description:
Petri Dish
This game has been made for the GDTV Game Jam 2025.
About
Petri Dish is a simple click to move game where you move around a petri dish as a cell and feed to split and evolve.
The aim of the game is to try to build resistance to either hot, cold or toxic regions of the petri dish.  Resistance can only be gained by evolving through reproduction.
To gain some resistance, you need to reproduce when affected by a region.
Story
You exist as a cell in a Petri Dish being experimented upon.  The experiment is cruel and tests your survival against a series of hazards.  Your only method of survival is evolution.  You need to evolve a resistance to the hazards, otherwise you will die and be considered as another failed experiment.
Credits
All art, music, sounds and code has been made by myself.
The font used in the game is "KenVectorThin2" from Kenny.
Other
This game has been made in about 20 hours of work for the jam.  I do not know if I will update or continue this project as it is a small, self-contained game made solely for the jam.
As always, I would appreciate any feedback you might have.
Minor update 26/05/2025 13:00 - Added capability to load instructions at menu and during gameplay by pressing "I", also added Instructions button to Pause Menu.  Change suggested by @TheWrongJohn - Thanks for the suggestion.
Buy Me A Coffee ... Please ...

details.title: Pineroot Hollow
details.description:
This game was developed for the GameDev.tv Game Jam 2025 based on the theme "Tiny World." 
Edit: spoilers ahead Some people have mentioned they were having some trouble finding the potion bottle, so we have added a screenshot to the game page so people know what they are looking for. It's small and yellow :- )
IMPORTANT: For the web-based version, you need hardware acceleration enabled in browser. The game is best played with the Windows build due to better rendering. But if you are not comfortable downloading the game, play in browser, but just keep in mind the vibrancy is slightly duller.
🌿Welcome to Pineroot Hollow🌿
✨A cozy, curious adventure in a world too small to notice... until now.
🌲One step too close to a mysterious bag, and poof—you’re no taller than a thimble.  
🍎Explore a hidden village tucked in the trunk of a great pine tree, where gnome folk chitter chatter, fruit houses glow with a soft golden warmth from within the trees, and stories sprout like wildflowers.
🍄Led by a welcoming mushroom guide and cheered on by the Hollowkin, you'll embark on a heartwarming quest to gather ingredients for a potion that could restore your size… or change your fate forever.
🌱 Shrink down. Slow down. See the world from a different angle. 🍂Pineroot Hollow is waiting for you.🍂
Controls:
WASD = move
Mouse = camera/menu control
E = interact
P = pause (Esc or P in desktop)
Space = continue dialogue or confirm dialogue selection
W/S = selection in dialogue

details.description:
UPDATE: A post-jam version of the game is available where I took some of the player feedback and made some changes like a randomized path for each playthrough. If you are interested, you can find it here:https://waijee.itch.io/the-world-tortoise-and-the-hare-championship-editionGame submission for GameDev.tv Game Jam 2025 where the theme was "Tiny World".

CONTROLS & GAMEPLAY:
Movement: Arrow Keys or WASDAction: Enter or Spacebar
Through the actions of the people that live on the back of the Tortoise, help steer the World Tortoise along the path so that she can win the race against the Hare. They may need to move to one side of the world to make the World Tortoise steer in that direction.




STORY:
The World Tortoise has been challenged by the jerk Hare to a foot race. The people of the World will need to help the World Tortoise navigate the path as she has bad vision. It will be a challenge that requires everyone's help.


From The Developer:Thanks for checking out my game. Thanks to my family who put up with me for the past week as I got this game built.
My goal with this year's project was to focus on creating a unique and original game. The technical skill I wanted to focus on in particular was polish. I'm happy with some of what I learned. There's still more I could have done like adding in more sound effects and thinking of a better game, but I like how the game fit the theme. 10 days may seem like a lot, but there's still only so many hours I was able to put into the game.
I hope you enjoy what I have made.

game.short_text: Dredge meets Disco Elysium - Explore the waterway, uncover the mystery.
game.title: Still Waters

details.title: Still Waters
details.description:
You are not the first to sail these waters...
And you won’t be the last to lose something to them.
A half-submerged island, untouched by time and map. A network of waterways riddled with the remnants of lost expeditions, drifting memories, and something whispering beneath the surface.
You’ve come here with a task:
Relight what has gone dark. Reach the heart of the river. Cast your darkest memory into the abyss. And above all—give. Even when nothing returns. For in this place, generosity guides the light.
Explore a Forgotten River
Navigate your small vessel through branching paths, eerie ruins, and forgotten mysteries. Every fork hides a story.
Light the Way
Engage in a simple-but-intriguing light rekindling minigame—each one different, yet familiar. Each beacon stands as a fragile foothold against the darkness.
Dredge meets Disco Elysium
Encounter strange Points of Interest: skeletal fishermen, drifting historians, weeping docks. Converse with ghosts, memories, and madness through richly written branching dialogue inspired by Disco Elysium.
No Combat. Just Choices.
Every POI offers a small quest. Click. Interact. Decide. No skill trees, no weapons. Just consequences and light.
INSTRUCTIONS:Solve each light tower puzzle to power the next one. (Start with the first—you can’t skip it!)

Follow the swaying power lines to navigate deeper into the river and find the Heart.

Meet strange characters and choose how to treat them—with generosity or cruelty.

Collect Scrap and Expedition Logs to upgrade your ship at the Drydock.

Still Waters is a narrative exploration puzzle game where you captain a lone vessel through a river lost to time and memory. Light your path forward using your wits, a series of corrupted light towers, and a quiet sense of resolve.
 
But it’s not cleverness that reveals the truth of these waters—it’s generosity. Only by giving freely, even when the river gives nothing in return, will you uncover the deeper mysteries hidden in the fog.
 
At the journey’s end, one final choice waits. One that will shape your story—and what remains of you—forever.


CONTROLS:

details.title: Linked Fate
details.description:
🌱 Linked Fate: MicroverseSurvivors
A 2D co-op roguelike where spirits are tethered by magic and survive together in a fragile micro-world.
 
🧬 Description
You are fragments of a forgotten guardian spirit, awakened within a crumbling shard of the Microverse. Bound together by a glowing tether, you must work as one to survive waves of invasive threats, protect your shared core, and reclaim the last spark of your tiny world.
 
Only unity will keep you alive. Only fate will decide your upgrades.
 
🕹️ Features
  👾 Local Co-op: 2–4 players, gamepad + keyboard support
   🔗 Tether Combat: Deal damage through your magical connection
   💔 Shared Health: One pool, one fate
   🧠 Character Classes: Choose from unique shapes with distinct stats and passives
   🌊 Wave Survival: Face off against escalating swarms of corrupted micro-creatures ✨ Tiny World Vibes: Glowing particles
🎮 Controls
🔹 Character Selection
 
  Press Space (Keyboard) or Start (Gamepad) to join
   Use Y / ▲  to view stats
   Press B / ⭕ / ESC to cancel selection
  
🔹 In-Game

⚠️ Requirements

🔧 Notes

game.short_text: You are the owner of a cozy little shop that specializes in creating custom terrariums for tiny creatures!
game.title: Tiny Company

details.title: Tiny Company
details.description:
Thanks to popular demand, the game is now available to wishlist on STEAM!
 And guess what? The full version is coming soon — with even more ways to play: Story, Daily, and Endless modes!
 
Get ready for an upgraded experience with new characters, extra items, fresh dialogue, and plenty more surprises. There’ll be daily challenges to keep things interesting, and an Endless Mode where you can build as many terrariums as your heart desires — and compete for that top score!
 
So come join the fun and let’s keep growing together — now on Steam!

In Tiny Company, you are the owner of a cozy little shop that specializes in creating custom terrariums. But unlike most stores, your clients are not people — they’re the actual future inhabitants of the terrariums! From ants and beetles to snakes, geckos, mantises, snails, and more, each visitor arrives with a specific vision for their terrarium dream home, and it’s your job to bring it to life.
 

In the full release version of the game, there are 3 different game modes you can choose from and have lots of fun:
• STORY Mode Each client comes in person to your shop, greets you with a short dialogue, and provides a reference photo of the terrarium they’d like you to build. You must recreate their desired terrarium layout using the decorative elements available in your shop. Try to match the reference as closely as possible to get the highest score, trigger unique dialogue reactions, or unlock the next level.
 
• DAILY Mode In this mode, you’ll receive orders online as daily challenges. Complete them to maintain your track record and hold your place — or advance — on the leaderboard.
 
• ENDLESS Mode Here, you’ll keep receiving online orders and can complete as many as you like. Have fun, get creative, and design countless unique terrariums. This mode also features a scoring system to help you compete for a top spot on the leaderboard.
 
 Using your mouse, click on boxes, sacks, or piles of sticks to drop items onto the counter. Then, pick up each item, hover it over the terrarium to position it, and let it drop inside. But there’s a catch: once you place an object, you can’t move it again. Also, items may have different properties, making interactions more fun and slightly more challenging.

In Story Mode, after completing a terrarium, your design is evaluated for accuracy and creativity, with a final score between 0 and 3 stars. Depending on how well you’ve captured the client’s vision, their reactions will vary — sometimes sweet, sometimes disappointed. As you earn stars, you’ll unlock new levels with different creatures, each presenting fresh decorative challenges. You can always revisit previous levels to aim for a better score.
 
In Daily and Endless modes, the gameplay remains the same, but the scoring system is different: you earn points that let you compete against other players for a place on the leaderboard.

Why Did the Chicken Cross the Globe? is a goofy 3D puzzle-platformer inspired by Super Mario 64.
You play as Popcorn, a clumsy wizard's apprentice who has accidentally transformed themself into a wide-eyed, frantic little chicken. To undo the spell and return to your human form, you must prove you’ve learned your lesson by jumping into the magic snow globes in your master wizard's study.
 
Each globe is a tiny world with three types of challenges: 🐣 Egg Collection – Track down hidden eggs scattered throughout the world. 🧠 Puzzle Solving – Use your wits (and telekinetic powers!) to solve the puzzle. 🪵 Platforming Trials – Glide, leap, and bounce your way across the terrain full of wind tunnels, launchpads, moving platforms and more.
 
Armed with telekinesis and a gliding jump. Use your tiny chicken brain to utilize telekinetic objects, pressure plates, and more to outwit magical mechanisms to find the scrolls to return to your human form.

Will : GitHubI'm Will, originally from the US but now I live in Poland. I have my degree in Mathematics and have been a traditional artist professionally for 13 years. game.short_text: Hop your grass off and keep collecting energy bits to keep the birds at bay...or become lunch!
game.title: Bird Snack Simulator

details.title: Bird Snack Simulator
details.description:
Take control of a grasshopper and use your perfectly aimed jumping skills to gather energy bits and keep away the birds as long as possible! 
Left Mouse Button: Jump strength
Right Mouse Button: Rotate Camera
E: Nudge/Short-Hop/Unstuck

HOW TO PLAY:
1) Hold Left Mouse Button to charge and aim jump (Right Mouse Button rotates camera).

2) Short (blue) jumps are more vertical.

3) Long (red) jumps are lower, with more distance.

4) Collect energy bits to keep the birds away!

5) The higher elevation the energy bit, the better the point and time bonus.

6) Jumping through rings also grants a great point and bird-away-time bonus.

7) Some critters may disrupt you, but they cause you no damage.

Stay alive as long as possible, and set some high scores!
---
Short jumps will give you a more vertical hop, while longer, stronger jumps will go for low and distant. As long as you are collecting energy bits or jumping through rings, the birds will stay at bay. See if you can set your own personal high score, longest survival time, or even best hangtime!
Energy bits that are at higher-up elevations are worth more points, as well as award more time on the "stay away, birds!" timer. Though riskier and more difficult to get to, their reward is well worth it. Jumping through rings also provides a huge score and timer bonus, but can be difficult and frustrating to hit correctly! Don't waste too much time trying!
You'll meet some other critters during your adventure, but don't worry, they'll only get in your way. The only thing you have to fear are the birds, so keep collecting as long as you can!
This game was made for the GameDev.tv Game Jam 2025.

game.short_text: "Fake it till you froth it." >> Visual Novel game for Gamedev.tv Game Jam 2025<<
game.title: Bean There, Brewed That

details.title: Bean There, Brewed That
details.description:
Bean There, Brewed That
A cozy, comedic visual novel about barista life, coffee chaos, and quirky coworkers.
 
☕ About the Game
Mina just started her first shift at a charming little café—and things are already getting weird. Whether it’s dealing with customers, navigating coworkers, or figuring out how not to set off the espresso machine again, every moment is steeped in a little bit of caffeine and a lot of character.
 
This short visual novel focuses on fun dialogue, silly moments, and laid-back vibes. There are no puzzles and no complex branching, but your choices still matter: the game features three possible endings based on how Mina handles her whirlwind shift.
 
🎨 Art & Sprite Credits
  Mina (Evelyn Sprite/CHIBI) – chibi785.itch.io
   Kara (Amber Sprite/CHIBI) – chibi785.itch.io
   Gerald (Alex Sprite) – eufasy.itch.io
   Pickles Sprite – doublefree.itch.io
  
🖼️ Backgrounds & Other Art
  Bedroom & Kitchen – spiralatlas.itch.io
   Café (outside & interior) – itsu-saragi.itch.io
   Café Counter – chibi785.itch.io
   Stock Images (Pixabay):
   Coffee Machine – StockSnap
   Coffee Grounds – Elias Shariff Falla Mardini
   Milk Frother – David Stephanus
     Other Café Assets – najmetender.itch.io
  
🎛️ GUI & Fonts
  GUI: Designed by Amanda Litwin using Free Canva and GIMP (open source)
   Fonts: Averia and Averia Libre by Dan Sayers (i@iotic.com)
  
🎵 Music & Sound Effects
  Opening Theme ("Tears Like Rain") – Golden Sound Labs via Pixabay
   Background Themes / Ambience – Golden Sound Labs, Lobsang Karma, freesound_community
   Sound Effects – Matthew Vakalyuk, floraphonic, freesound_community via Pixabay
  
More detailed Credits also listed at the end of the game.   I appreciate everyone of these creators and artists and could not have done it without them. <3 Show them love.
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
🌍 Made in 10 Days for a Game Jam
This visual novel was created in just 10 days for the GameDev.tv Game Jam, inspired by the theme "Tiny World."
 
The idea of a “tiny world” sparked the concept of a cozy café where everything—from your first job jitters to frothing milk—feels like the biggest thing in the universe. It’s a story about learning, growing, and trying to keep it together when your world is small but your challenges feel huge.
 
Built with Ren'Py and a blend of free and open-source assets, this project was created from scratch during the jam as a lighthearted take on those small moments that mean everything (and because I just had to get a job myself and so that was my inspiration.)
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
💌 Thanks for playing! This project was brewed with love and extra espresso. If it made you smile, let us know!
Want to see more stories like this?
Your support means the world — and helps make more interactive, choice-driven experiences possible.

✨ Support the journey on Patreon: patreon.com/TeharraRoseProductions

game.short_text: A point and click puzzle game I made for the GameDev.tv 2025 game jam
game.title: Tiny Big Quest

details.title: Tiny Big Quest
details.description:
You play as Tania, as she shrinks in size into an unknown world!
Meet the "Dust Bunny" people, explore a village made of bread, croissant and donuts, solve a dungeon puzzle and restore magic to this tiny realm!
As a young woman who is moving out and decided to do a last check in the attic, she barely knew she was about to go on a Tiny Big Quest of her own!
Controls: Use the left mouse button to look for hotspots and interact, and the right mouse button to call the inventory forth. (but it's ok if you can't 'right click', the game is set so you can complete just by interacting with stuff)
----
This is a project I went solo, for the GameDev.tv 2025 jam...
The theme was "Tiny World" and I came up with this idea for an adventure game, after seeing the sponsored assets...
Took me around 7 days to finish the core coding + art and the other 3 days for polishing. 
I used 'Adventure Game Studio' (AGS) engine, and for the art I used  'Blender', 'Aseprite', 'PixelOver' (pixel art shader for 3D),' Pixel Composer' (pixel art particle engine), LDtk (for assembling tilesets).
------
